title: lycium salsum var. gracile
intro: Lycium salsum var. gracile, also known as the Patagonian boxthorn, is a plant species that belongs to the Solanaceae family. It is a shrub-like plant native to the Patagonia region, valued for its medicinal properties and health benefits. The plant contains flavonoids and phenolic acids, which have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, and it is traditionally used to treat various health conditions, including high blood pressure, diabetes, and cardiovascular diseases. Lycium salsum var. gracile is often used in herbal preparations, such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, and is also consumed as a supplement in capsule or powder form.
